# Keyword Guidance

Topic-specific primary and secondary keyword lists for each chapter, plus general rules.

---

## General Rules

1. **Audience vocabulary first**: Semi-technical leaders (CTOs, VPs, engineering managers) search in plain English, not academic jargon. Prefer "how AI agents work" over "multi-agent orchestration framework."
2. **Full forms win**: "Retrieval-Augmented Generation" gets more search traffic than "RAG" alone. Use the full form in the title and first heading; the acronym is fine thereafter.
3. **Action-oriented secondary terms**: Include terms that signal decision intent: "when to use", "vs", "trade-offs", "for business", "explained", "guide".
4. **Never suggest**: keyword density ratios, hidden text, or any term not already reflected in the chapter content.

---

## Chapter 1 — Foundations of AI

| Type | Keywords |
|---|---|
| Primary | `artificial intelligence explained` / `what is AI` |
| Secondary | `AI history`, `AI vs machine learning`, `symbolic AI`, `AI for business leaders`, `Turing Test`, `types of AI` |
| Avoid | Generic "AI" alone in headings — too broad |

---

## Chapter 2 — Machine Learning

| Type | Keywords |
|---|---|
| Primary | `machine learning explained` / `how machine learning works` |
| Secondary | `supervised learning`, `unsupervised learning`, `reinforcement learning`, `ML algorithms`, `machine learning for managers`, `training data` |
| Avoid | "ML" acronym in the title without expansion |

---

## Chapter 3 — Deep Learning

| Type | Keywords |
|---|---|
| Primary | `deep learning explained` / `neural networks explained` |
| Secondary | `convolutional neural network`, `CNN`, `RNN`, `LSTM`, `deep learning vs machine learning`, `backpropagation`, `activation functions` |
| Avoid | Overusing "neural network" without context — readers confuse it with the brain analogy |

---

## Chapter 4 — Transformers

| Type | Keywords |
|---|---|
| Primary | `Transformer architecture explained` / `attention mechanism AI` |
| Secondary | `self-attention`, `BERT`, `GPT architecture`, `encoder decoder`, `positional encoding`, `how transformers work` |
| Full form | "Transformer" (capital T) = the architecture; always spell out on first use |

---

## Chapter 5 — Large Language Models

| Type | Keywords |
|---|---|
| Primary | `large language models explained` / `LLMs explained` |
| Secondary | `GPT-4`, `Claude`, `Llama`, `LLM training`, `prompt engineering`, `scaling laws`, `emergent abilities`, `LLM for business` |
| Full form | "Large Language Model (LLM)" on first use |

---

## Chapter 6 — Generative AI

| Type | Keywords |
|---|---|
| Primary | `generative AI explained` / `what is generative AI` |
| Secondary | `diffusion models`, `GANs`, `image generation AI`, `Stable Diffusion`, `DALL-E`, `generative AI for business`, `AI content generation` |
| Avoid | "GenAI" acronym without prior expansion |

---

## Chapter 7 — Embeddings & Vector Databases

| Type | Keywords |
|---|---|
| Primary | `embeddings AI explained` / `vector databases explained` |
| Secondary | `semantic search`, `vector search`, `Pinecone`, `Weaviate`, `Chroma`, `text embeddings`, `cosine similarity`, `embedding models` |
| Full form | "vector database" — spell out, not "vector DB" in headings |

---

## Chapter 8 — RAG Systems

| Type | Keywords |
|---|---|
| Primary | `retrieval augmented generation explained` / `RAG AI explained` |
| Secondary | `RAG pipeline`, `document retrieval AI`, `grounded AI responses`, `RAG vs fine-tuning`, `chunking strategies`, `hybrid retrieval`, `AI knowledge base` |
| Full form | "Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)" on first use — "RAG" alone is ambiguous |

---

## Chapter 9 — Fine-Tuning & PEFT

| Type | Keywords |
|---|---|
| Primary | `fine-tuning LLMs explained` / `how to fine-tune AI models` |
| Secondary | `LoRA`, `QLoRA`, `PEFT`, `parameter efficient fine-tuning`, `RLHF`, `instruction tuning`, `DPO`, `fine-tuning vs RAG` |
| Full form | "Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT)", "Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF)" |

---

## Chapter 10 — Multimodal AI

| Type | Keywords |
|---|---|

| Secondary | `GPT-4V`, `CLIP`, `image understanding AI`, `audio AI`, `video AI`, `multimodal LLM`, `visual question answering` |
| Full form | "vision-language model" — spell out before introducing acronym VLM |

| Type | Keywords |
|---|---|

| Type | Keywords |
|---|---|

## Chapter 13 — AI Infrastructure

| Type | Keywords |
|---|---|
| Primary | `AI infrastructure explained` / `deploying AI models` |
| Secondary | `vLLM`, `model quantization`, `GPU inference`, `distributed training`, `AI serving`, `model optimization`, `TensorRT` |

## Chapter 14 — System Design

| Type | Keywords |
|---|---|
| Primary | `AI system design` / `building AI applications architecture` |
| Secondary | `LLM caching`, `AI monitoring`, `AI architecture patterns`, `prompt caching`, `AI latency`, `AI cost optimization`, `production AI` |
| Avoid | Generic "system design" without "AI" — too broad |

---

## Chapter 15 — Glossary

| Type | Keywords |
|---|---|
| Primary | `AI glossary` / `AI terms explained` |
| Secondary | `machine learning glossary`, `LLM glossary`, `AI terminology`, `AI dictionary`, `AI concepts A to Z` |
| Note | Glossary should use `<dt>` / `<dd>` or strong headings per term to enable search engine term-level indexing |
